libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image, environment variable, or system property is indicated. Tests should still exercise resources from the target third-party artifact directly, such as goog/caja, goog/dojo, goog/mochikit, goog/osapi, and goog/silverlight paths, rather than counting core Closure resources as coverage of the third-party jar.

deterministic_setup.reason: The third-party jar declares extensions to Google Closure Library and omits core resources like goog/base.js and goog/deps.js in 0.0-1376-2; the sibling google-closure-library jar provides those core resources for normal use.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8743 [Automation] java run fails for org.clojure:google-closure-library-third-party:0.0-1376-2; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Adding the companion core Closure jar can make classpath resource checks pass even when they are not exercising the target third-party artifact; tests should distinguish companion core resources from third-party extension resources.
Local Gradle and Native Image verification remain authoritative because this artifact is resource-only and classpath/resource packaging behavior is the main failure mode.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: The 0.0-1376-2 third-party artifact is a split companion jar: it contains only third-party Closure extension resources, while core Closure resources such as goog/base.js and goog/deps.js are in the sibling org.clojure:google-closure-library artifact. Meaningful tests that model normal Closure usage need that companion jar on the test classpath.
